No Poverty.reason: The course focuses on business efficiency and technology in supply chains, with no direct or strong indirect links to poverty eradication or economic resources for the poor.

Zero Hunger.reason: There is no mention of food security, agriculture, malnutrition, or sustainable food production systems in the course content.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course syllabus contains no evidence related to health services, disease prevention, well-being, or safety in a health context.

Quality Education.reason: The course explicitly aims for students to 'acquire advanced professional knowledge' (Target 4.3/4.4) and 'understand sustainability considerations' (Target 4.7), directly contributing to quality education.
evidence: 習得相關進階專業知識 (Students will acquire and apply expertise in their respective fields.)
理解與辨別商業環境中道德、社會責任與永續議題 (Students will understand and be able to identify the ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability considerations of business situations.)

Gender Equality.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus relates to gender-specific issues, discrimination, or empowerment.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: The course content does not mention water quality, access to sanitation, or related infrastructure.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: There is no direct evidence regarding energy access, renewable energy, or energy efficiency as a primary focus of the course.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course focuses on 'Intelligent Supply Chain' and 'Industry 4.0', directly supporting technological upgrading and innovation for economic productivity (Target 8.2) and overall economic growth.
evidence: Intelligent Supply Chain: Trends and Applications
立足於在準確性(Accuracy)、速度 (Speed) 和質量 (Quality) 方面展開市場競爭

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course's core themes of 'Intelligent Supply Chain,' 'Big Data and Manufacturing (Industry 4.0),' and 'Big Data and Logistics' directly align with upgrading infrastructure, industrialization, technology, and innovation (Targets 9.1, 9.2, 9.4, 9.5).
evidence: 智能供應鏈：趨勢與運用 (Intelligent Supply Chain: Trends and Applications)

Reduced Inequalities.reason: No explicit or strong inferred links to income inequality, social inclusion, or discriminatory practices are present in the course material.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: The 'Big Data and Logistics' topic has an inferred, albeit indirect, connection to improving transport systems (Target 11.2), which can contribute to urban sustainability.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The course's focus on 'Intelligent Supply Chain' inherently promotes efficient resource use (Target 12.2) and waste reduction. The 'sustainability considerations' core competency directly links to Target 12.6.
evidence: 理解與辨別商業環境中道德、社會責任與永續議題 (Students will understand and be able to identify the ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability considerations of business situations.)

Climate Action.reason: While efficient logistics and manufacturing (Industry 4.0) can indirectly reduce emissions, climate action is not explicitly mentioned or a primary focus of the course.

Life Below Water.reason: There is no evidence in the course syllabus related to marine ecosystems, pollution, or sustainable fisheries.

Life on Land.reason: The course content does not address terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, deforestation, or land degradation.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: No explicit or strong inferred links to governance, justice, rule of law, or institutional strength are found in the syllabus.

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course's discussion of 'Supplier Networks' and 'shared supply network' directly implies multi-stakeholder partnerships and knowledge sharing (Targets 17.6, 17.17) within a business context.
evidence: 共享的供應網絡中實現更多的情境智能 (Contextual Intelligence)
